title: Feature: Complete ADR-2603240130 — Declarative Swarm Agent Behavior from YAML

done: agent_def.rs — full AgentDefinition serde structs (ModelConfig, ContextConfig, WorkflowConfig, FeedbackLoopConfig, QualityThresholds, TokenBudget)
model_selection.rs — select_from_yaml() with preferred/fallback/upgrade logic + full test coverage
workflow_engine.rs — execute_phases(), run_feedback_gates(), run_feedback_loop() with placeholder resolution
supervisor.rs — YAML model selection wired for hex-coder (both branches), hex-reviewer, hex-tester
supervisor.rs — feedback gate failure → FixAgent → retry gates

steps.description: Add execute_step_for_phase() to CodePhase — phase-aware inference that accepts a phase name and injects the phase's YAML steps into the system prompt as TDD instructions. Phases: pre_validate (boundary check only, no inference), red (write failing tests), green (write implementation), refactor (clean up).
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; CodePhase::execute_step_for_phase compiles and has unit test for prompt injection

steps.description: Wire pre_validate gate enforcement in dispatch_agent for hex-coder: before any inference call, run the boundary_check gate from the workflow engine. If the gate fails (blocking=true), set the step as failed and return early — do not proceed to code generation.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; test that a failing boundary_check gate prevents execute_step from being called

steps.description: Replace build_coder_context with build_context_from_yaml in dispatch_agent for hex-coder. Pass target_adapter and current_edit_file from the workplan step. Falls back to build_coder_context only if agent has no load_strategy.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; AgentContext.metadata contains context_source=yaml for hex-coder steps

steps.description: Replace hardcoded MAX_FILE_BYTES (4096) with per-agent token budget from YAML. Read context.token_budget.max from the loaded AgentDefinition in read_file_truncated. Fall back to 4096 only when no YAML budget is defined. MAX_FILE_BYTES becomes a fallback constant, not the universal cap.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; unit test: agent with token_budget.max=2000 truncates files at 2000 bytes

steps.description: Wire phase-driven inference in dispatch_agent for hex-coder: replace the single execute_step call with sequential per-phase calls using execute_step_for_phase() from step-1. Phases: pre_validate (skip inference, gate only) → red → green → refactor. Each phase call gets the phase steps from YAML injected as system prompt context.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; integration test shows 3 distinct inference calls (red/green/refactor) in trace logs for a coder step

steps.description: Wire quality threshold enforcement in dispatch_agent: after code generation completes for a step, call evaluate_quality_thresholds for the hex-coder role. Log all violations. For blocking violations (max_file_lines, max_function_lines exceeded by >50%), mark the step as needing fix and invoke FixAgent.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; evaluate_quality_thresholds called in hex-coder dispatch after code write; violations logged with tracing::warn

steps.description: Wire agent cardinality from swarm YAML in the pipeline orchestration. Read cardinality from dev-pipeline.yml for each role (per_workplan_step, per_source_file, per_tier, per_swarm). Implement cardinality resolution: per_workplan_step = one dispatch per step (current behavior, make explicit); per_source_file = one dispatch per generated file; per_tier = one dispatch per tier; per_swarm = one dispatch total.
steps.done_condition: cargo check passes; SwarmComposition::cardinality_for_role() returns correct enum variant; supervisor reads and logs cardinality for each role

steps.description: Integration test: run hex dev against a minimal Rust feature (hello-world CLI) and verify the full YAML-driven pipeline executes end-to-end. Assert: model used matches hex-coder.yml preferred, all 5 workflow phases execute, feedback gates run, no fallback to hardcoded behavior. Write test to tests/integration/declarative_swarm_test.rs.
steps.done_condition: cargo test declarative_swarm_test passes; test verifies YAML model ID appears in step result

mergeOrder: step-1, step-2, step-3 — parallel (independent tier-0 changes to different functions)
step-4 after step-3 (reads context config from same agent def)
step-5 after step-1 + step-2 (requires execute_step_for_phase + pre_validate gate)
step-6 after step-5 (quality check runs after phase-driven code gen)
step-7 after step-3 (cardinality reads same swarm YAML already loaded)
step-8 last (integration test validates all prior steps)

riskRegister.risk: Phase-driven inference (step-5) triples inference call count per step — token cost increases 3x for hex-coder
riskRegister.mitigation: Gate phase dispatch behind a feature flag (HEX_PHASE_MODE=tdd|single); default to single until cost/quality tradeoff is validated

riskRegister.risk: execute_step_for_phase may produce inconsistent code across phases if context drifts between red/green/refactor calls
riskRegister.mitigation: Pass full accumulated file content as context to each subsequent phase; green phase receives red phase output

riskRegister.risk: Token budget enforcement (step-4) may truncate critical port interfaces, breaking code gen
riskRegister.mitigation: Prioritize port_interfaces over source_files when applying budget; never truncate ports below 1000 bytes

riskRegister.risk: pre_validate gate may false-positive on Tier 0 (domain/ports have no adapters to import)
riskRegister.mitigation: Skip boundary_check gate for tier=0 steps; only enforce for tier >= 1

successCriteria: hex dev <feature> uses the model from hex-coder.yml preferred field (verified in trace logs)
Three distinct OpenRouter inference calls appear per code step (red/green/refactor) when HEX_PHASE_MODE=tdd
A boundary violation in a Tier-1 step causes pre_validate to abort code gen — no wasted inference call
Feedback gate failure (compile error) triggers FixAgent and gates re-run once after fix
cargo test passes with zero new failures after all steps are merged
